channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal freeze on energy and climate funding causing concern, confusion in Minnesota
item.description: Some of the money has been released since federal judges ordered the funding to be unfrozen. But weatherization programs and rebates for home efficiency upgrades are still on hold. State and local officials say a lack of communication has led to confusion and uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: USAID workers will be given 15 minutes to clear their workspaces as the agency gets dismantled
item.description: Thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development workers who have been fired or placed on leave as part of the Trump administration’s dismantling of the agency are being given a brief window Thursday and Friday to clear out their workspaces.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration says it's cutting 90% of USAID foreign aid contracts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration said it is eliminating more than 90% of the U.S. Agency for International Development’s foreign aid contracts and $60 billion in overall U.S. assistance around the world, putting numbers on its plans to eliminate the majority of U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MARK SHERMAN and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GPS navigation problems among topics addressed by bills at Nevada Legislature
item.description: GPS navigation is handy, but it's not perfect. When road closures or hazards aren't updated, technology isn't helping anyone.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VA pauses billions in cuts lauded by Musk as lawmakers and veterans decry loss of critical care
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs has temporarily suspended billions of dollars in contract cuts after an uproar that critical veterans health services were harmed, lawmakers and veterans service organizations said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawmakers differ over location of Florida’s Black history museum
item.description: Florida has a rich and long history involving contributions from its Black residents. There are areas across the state highlighting those struggles and efforts. Some of those key moments are displayed at the Robert W Saunders, Sr Public Library in Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: National parks already feel the effects of layoffs, even before the busy season starts
item.description: Some 1,000 NPS employees were fired, and hiring for seasonal positions was delayed. Here’s what to know about the impacts already being felt at parks, and what it could mean for the busy season.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s social media video garners pushback from Arabs and Muslims in U.S. and Gaza
item.description: In a seemingly AI-generated video that the president posted on social media, images of destruction due to the war in Gaza are transformed into a glitzy resort called “TRUMP GAZA.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, Hadeel Al-Shalchi, and Anas Baba, MPR News

item.title: GOP rep reveals after F-16 ride why Trump's leadership is already paying off for military
item.description: Fox News Digital spoke to GOP Rep. Abe Hamadeh about military readiness after he toured Luke Air Force Base in Arizona and flew in an F-16 fighter jet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OPM instructs agencies to turn over plans for mass government layoffs
item.description: A Wednesday memo from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) and Office of Management and Budget (OMB) directs agencies across government to turn over plans for widespread layoffs of federal employees by March 13. The memo, which provides more specific guidance after a Feb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pushes DOGE, tariffs at first Cabinet meeting
item.description: Musk said the U.S. cannot continue with $2 trillion deficits and huge amounts of interest on the national debt.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams asks to have charges dropped after leaked letter
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyers filed a motion to have his charges dropped after someone “leaked” a letter from now-resigned U.S. Attorney Danielle Sasson, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Latvia FM: Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he couldn't in Ukraine war
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Latvia's foreign minister warns that Russian President Vladimir Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he hasn’t been able to accomplish in his war against Ukraine — weaken the United States and restore control over his smaller neighbor.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: With a push from President Donald Trump, House Republicans sent a GOP budget blueprint to passage Tuesday, a step toward delivering his “big, beautiful bill” with $4.5 trillion in tax breaks and $2 trillion in spending cuts despite a wall of opposition from Democrats and discomfort among Republicans
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rideshare drivers would get power to unionize under Minnesota bill that faces long road
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature passed a sweeping bill in 2024 giving rideshare drivers a pay raise. Now, some of the drivers want to unionize.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: How federal funding flows from the White House to Minnesotans
item.description: Amid the Trump administration’s slashes to program budgets from scientific research to education to social services, nonprofits and state agencies are left in financial and legal limbo.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellen Finn, MPR News

item.title: Federal watchdog agency intervenes in Trump’s purge of probationary employees
item.description: U.S. Special Counsel Hampton Dellinger has asked the Merit Systems Protection Board to temporarily reinstate six federal employees fired from their jobs and is considering ways to seek relief for others.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Reconciliation is the key to unlocking Trump’s agenda. Here’s how it works
item.description: Republicans in Congress are turning to a tool of the budget process to pass major elements of President Trump’s agenda without the threat of a filibuster from Senate Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: UNLV testing cameras to read license plates in aftermath of deadly 2023 attack
item.description: Cameras that can read license plates are part of the security response that followed the Dec. 5, 2023, shooting on the UNLV campus. They are just part of what UNLV is doing in an effort to keep students safer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court schedules arguments in case where Trump administration is defending ACA
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday scheduled arguments for April 21 in a case that could decide the legality of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) requirement that insurers cover certain preventive services.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: St. Pete lawmaker proposes bill to dive into wastewater, sewage spill concerns
item.description: The devastating impacts of storm surge and flood waters are a harsh reality many Floridians know all too well.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As time change nears, Nevada considers opting out of daylight saving
item.description: Don't have a heart attack, but daylight saving time is less than two weeks away. If a Nevada lawmaker has her way, it will be the last time the state "springs ahead.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Union workers written up, fired for using sick days; Nevada lawmaker moves to close loophole
item.description: Some union workers are afraid to use sick days because they might be written up or fired for being "undependable," and now a Nevada lawmaker is coming to their aid.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis reappoints Moms for Liberty cofounder to Florida ethics commission
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is attempting to put Moms for Liberty cofounder Tina Descovich on the Florida Commission on Ethics for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge cancels trial for NYC Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cancelled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
